mnemonics: A TEST is for a Specific Time: Autocorrelation, Trend, Seasonality, and Stationarity are the key components to test for.
keyPoints: Tests determine if a time series is stationary (e.g., ADF test).
Tests identify the presence of trends and seasonality.
Tests (like the Ljung-Box) check if residuals are white noise, indicating a good model fit.
commonMistakes: Using tests without understanding the underlying assumptions (e.g., ADF test has different null hypotheses).
Ignoring test results that don't support a desired model (e.g., forcing a model on non-stationary data).
Applying a test meant for one type of data (e.g., a test for seasonality) to data with a different structure.
